etymology.description: 田(田) (tián, "field") + 力(力) (lì, "strength") = man providing the strength for working a field -> male; man
etymology.interpretation: The character 男(nán, "male; man") combines the fields where labor is performed with the strength needed for this work, reflecting traditional male roles in agriculture.

examples.chinese: 他是一位男士(他是一位男士)。
男孩喜欢玩耍(男孩喜歡玩耍)，男孩努力学习(男孩努力學習)。
examples.pinyin: Nánhái xǐhuān wánshuǎ, nánhái nǔlì xuéxí.
examples.english: Boys love to play, and boys study hard.

examples.chinese: 父母希望生个男(父母希望生個男)。
examples.english: The parents hope to have a son.
